description: A designed weather-safety infographic (NWS/NOAA) on a dark stormy background showing a photographic night sky with multiple bright lightning bolts striking. At top-left in white text: 'Lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun and can reach temperatures around.' Dominating the right is the very large white figure '50,000°F.' On the lower-left is an illustration of a thermometer with a red rising column over a cross-section of the layered, glowing orange/yellow interior of the Sun, with a small lightning-bolt icon at the bottom. A small orange/tan caption at bottom-right reads 'When Thunder Roars Go Indoors' with 'weather.gov/lightning' beneath it. This is a Lightning Safety Awareness Week public-service graphic emphasizing how hot lightning is to encourage people to seek shelter; target audience is the general public. Not immigration-related; produced under the prior administration.
notable_text: Lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun and can reach temperatures around 50,000°F; When Thunder Roars Go Indoors; weather.gov/lightning
